brief_justification: Arrives at exactly (A) with formal structure extraction and per-answer validation; extracts the abstract 0-1 knapsack template and traces why domain differences (continuity, subjectivity) are structurally irrelevant; explicitly names 'surface similarity bias' as the trap before analyzing, crediting the Suppress signal; rules out B/C/D with specific structural arguments constituting boundary condition checks; scopes conclusions precisely under the indivisibility assumption and flags domain intuition as the misleading factor; all four answer choices are evaluated with explicit rejection reasons; formal structure → mapping table → per-answer derivation is fully reproducible by a third party; explicitly avoids surface-only matching and names it as the suppressed failure mode; no evidence of surface similarity bias, domain lock, or structure blindness — suppressed modes are addressed by name; follows identify→extract→scan→map→verify path with parallel domain mapping mirroring the topology's FORK structure.
